creator: Alexander Terp
description:
## Motivation
Limited screen real estate problem: when you have multiple monitors and a laptop, you do not always want Kan taking up a full screen. The goal is a compact view you can pin to a narrow strip of screen - just enough to see your active tasks and process them quickly (advance, move, create). Full board detail is overkill for the "what am I working on right now?" workflow.

The key use case: glance at your current tasks, tick them off or move them as you work through the day, create quick cards as things come up. Detailed card editing (modal, custom fields) happens later on the full board.

## Core Concept
A new view mode called "Slim Mode" where board columns are stacked vertically instead of side-by-side. Designed for a narrow window (~300-400px wide) pinned to one edge of a screen. All interaction is optimized for quick task processing with minimal clicks.

## Layout
- Columns stacked vertically, each taking the full width of the narrow window
- All columns shown for v1 (no column filtering/selection). Rationale: simplest approach, and keyboard shortcuts (1-9) to jump between columns mitigate the scrolling concern. Column filtering is a natural v2 addition if scrolling becomes a pain.
- Scrollable vertically through all columns
- Column headers visible with card count
- Empty columns still shown with their header and a "new card" button/input

## Card Movement
Two mechanisms, optimized for different cases:

### Advance Button (Common Case - 1 Click)
- Each card has a visible advance button (checkmark or arrow icon)
- One click moves the card to the TOP of the next column
- "Next column" is implicit based on column display order (e.g. if columns are Today, Review, Done - advancing from Today goes to Review)
- No per-column configuration of advance destination for v1; always next in order
- This handles the 80% case: "I finished this task, move it forward"

### Right-Click Context Menu (Uncommon Case - 2 Interactions)
- Right-clicking a card opens a context menu listing ALL columns as move targets
- One click on a column in the menu moves the card there
- Handles cases like: send back to a previous column, skip to Done, move to a column not adjacent
- This is the escape hatch for the other 20% of moves

### Drag and Drop
- Drag and drop between stacked columns should be supported
- Reuses existing dnd-kit setup adapted for vertical layout
- This is a complement to the buttons, not a replacement - would be surprising to users if drag-and-drop stopped working in this mode

## Card Creation
- Number keys (1-9) scroll to the corresponding column and focus the card input, same as current behavior but adapted for vertical scrolling instead of horizontal
- Each column has a "new card" button/input area, same as current board view
- Cards are created as quick/lightweight entries - the expectation is you fill in details later on the full board

## Disabled in Slim Mode
These features are disabled because they are designed for wider viewports and would not work well in a narrow window:

- **Card edit modal**: The modal is a wide layout and would not fit or look good in ~300px. No attempt to resize the browser window or adapt the modal for v1. Implication: click-to-edit-card does not open the modal in slim mode (need to decide what it does instead - perhaps nothing, or just selects/highlights the card).
- **Shift+Enter floating field panel**: This panel appears to the right of newly created cards and there is no room for it. Shift+Enter should either do nothing or behave like regular Enter in slim mode.

The design philosophy: slim mode is for quick processing. Detailed editing happens on the full board.

## Navigation & Keyboard
- **Number keys (1-9)**: Scroll to and focus the corresponding column card input. Columns should display their number as a subtle badge so users know which number to press.
- **Omnibar (Cmd+K)**: Works normally. All existing functionality preserved - card search, board switching, slash commands, everything.
- **Omnibar arrow key navigation**: Up/down only for v1. In the current board, left/right arrows move between columns, but in slim mode everything is vertical. Up/down navigates through cards. Left/right column-jumping deferred to v2.
- **Board switching via omnibar**: When switching boards, STAY in slim mode. Do not revert to normal board view. The mode is a user preference, not a board property.

## Entry & Exit
All three of these should work:
- **Header toggle**: A button/icon in the header UI to switch between normal and slim mode
- **Keyboard shortcut**: TBD by implementing agent, but should exist
- **URL route**: /slim (or /slim appended to board URL - implementing agent to decide exact routing)
- **CLI flag**: kan serve --slim to launch directly into slim mode

## Persistence
- Slim mode on/off state stored in localStorage (not board config file)
- Rationale: this is a per-user display preference, not something to check into version control where it would affect other users of the same board
- Persists across board switches within a session
- Persists across page refreshes

## Column Limits
- Same behavior as current board view. If a destination column is at its card limit, block the move operation.
- No changes to column limit behavior - this is out of scope.

## Maintainability (Critical)
This is the highest priority non-functional requirement. Adding a new view mode risks creating a large maintenance surface area where changes to one mode break the other.

- **Layout variant, NOT a separate app**: Slim mode should be achieved through layout changes and conditional rendering on existing components, not by building parallel Board/Column/Card components.
- **Reuse existing components**: Card, Column components should be the same, extended with props or context to adjust their rendering for slim mode (e.g. stacked layout, showing advance button).
- **Same hooks**: useBoard, useFileSync, useOmnibar, etc. - no new state management layer.
- **Same API endpoints**: No backend changes needed. All card movement uses the same moveCard API that drag-and-drop already uses.
- **Shared code paths**: The implementing agent should actively look for ways to share rendering logic. If something requires a slim-mode-specific code path, that is a code smell worth examining.
- **Documentation**: Update ALL documentation, auxiliary files, help text, and any other references when implementing. This includes CLI help, web UI help/tooltips, and any docs pages.

## Out of Scope for v1
These are explicitly deferred. They are natural follow-ups but should NOT be part of the initial implementation:
- Column filtering (showing only selected columns instead of all)
- Custom advance destinations per column (always uses implicit next-column ordering)
- Card modal adaptation for slim mode
- Floating field panel in slim mode
- Left/right arrow key navigation in omnibar during slim mode
- Any backend/API changes
title: Slim Mode
